    "suggestionContent": "Invoice INSERT is missing tax and total columns. The SQL at line 81 only inserts `order_id` and `amount` (`INSERT INTO invoices (order_id, amount) VALUES ($1, $2)`), but the Invoice object created at lines 73-79 has `tax`, `total`, and `paidAt` fields. These values are computed but never persisted to the database. Any downstream system reading from the invoices table will see only the amount with no tax or total breakdown, making financial reconciliation and tax reporting impossible. Concrete scenario: accountant runs a tax report querying the invoices table and finds zero tax records because the tax column was never written.",

Concrete scenario: currentStock = 2, order.quantity = 5, newStock = -3. The system sets stock to -3 and proceeds to charge, invoice, and ship an order that cannot physically be fulfilled.",

    "suggestionContent": "Session token is returned in plaintext to the caller. At line 60, the `login` method returns `{ sessionId, userId, token, ... }` where `token` is the raw 32-byte hex token. The same raw token is also stored directly in the database at line 56-57. If any part of the call chain logs the return value or if the database is compromised, all session tokens are immediately usable. Best practice is to store a hashed version of the token in the database (e.g., `SHA-256(token)`) and return the raw token only to the client. 30:     async get<T>(key: string, fetchFn: () => Promise<T>, ttlMs: number = 60000): Promise<T> {
31:         const localEntry = this.localCache.get(key);
32:         if (localEntry && localEntry.expiresAt > Date.now()) {
33:             return localEntry.value as T;
34:         }
35: 
36:         const redisValue = await this.redis.get(key);
37:         if (redisValue) {
38:             const parsed = JSON.parse(redisValue) as T;
39:             this.localCache.set(key, { value: parsed, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });
40:             return parsed;
41:         }
42: 
43:         const freshValue = await fetchFn();
44:         await this.redis.set(key, JSON.stringify(freshValue), 'PX', ttlMs);
45:         this.localCache.set(key, { value: freshValue, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });
46:         return freshValue;
47:     }
48: 
49:     async invalidate(key: string): Promise<void> {
50:         await this.redis.del(key);
51:         this.logger.info(`Cache invalidated: ${key}`);
52:     }
53: 
54:     deepMerge(target: Record<string, any>, source: Record<string, any>): Record<string, any> {
55:         const result = { ...target };
56:         for (const key of Object.keys(source)) {
57:             if (typeof source[key] === 'object' && source[key] !== null && typeof result[key] === 'object') {
58:                 result[key] = this.deepMerge(result[key], source[key]);
59:             } else {
60:                 result[key] = source[key];
61:             }
62:         }
63:         return result;
64:     }
65: 
66:     async getConfig(configKey: string): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
67:         const defaults = await this.get(`config:defaults:${configKey}`, () =>
68:             this.db.query('SELECT value FROM config WHERE key = $1', [configKey])
69:         );
70:         const overrides = await this.get(`config:overrides:${configKey}`, () =>
71:             this.db.query('SELECT value FROM config_overrides WHERE key = $1', [configKey])
72:         );
73:         return this.deepMerge(defaults, overrides);

vars.suggestionsContext:
[
  {
    "id": "1",
    "suggestionContent": "Unbounded local cache growth causes memory leak. The `localCache` Map (line 8) grows without limit. Every unique cache key adds an entry, and entries are only logically expired (checked at read time via `expiresAt`) but never physically removed. Over time, the Map accumulates all keys ever cached. For a system caching per-user or per-request data, this means millions of entries consuming memory indefinitely. The `invalidate` method (lines 49-52) only deletes from Redis, not from localCache. The `get` method (line 31-34) returns stale entries only if not expired, but never deletes expired entries it encounters.",
    "existingCode": "        const localEntry = this.localCache.get(key);\n        if (localEntry && localEntry.expiresAt > Date.now()) {\n            return localEntry.value as T;\n        }",
    "improvedCode": "        const localEntry = this.localCache.get(key);\n        if (localEntry) {\n            if (localEntry.expiresAt > Date.now()) {\n                return localEntry.value as T;\n            }\n            this.localCache.delete(key);\n        }",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Memory leak -- expired local cache entries are never deleted, causing unbounded Map growth over time",
    "relevantLinesStart": 31,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 34,
    "label": "performance",
    "severity": "high"
  },
  {
    "id": "2",
    "suggestionContent": "Unhandled JSON.parse error on corrupted Redis data. Line 38 calls `JSON.parse(redisValue)` without a try/catch. If the Redis value is corrupted (partial write, encoding issue, manual tampering), `JSON.parse` throws a `SyntaxError` which propagates up and crashes the caller. Since this is a cache layer, a parse failure should be treated as a cache miss -- the system should fall through to `fetchFn()` and re-populate the cache with valid data. Concrete scenario: Redis returns a truncated JSON string due to a connection reset during write; the entire application request fails instead of gracefully falling back to the source.",
    "existingCode": "        const redisValue = await this.redis.get(key);\n        if (redisValue) {\n            const parsed = JSON.parse(redisValue) as T;\n            this.localCache.set(key, { value: parsed, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });\n            return parsed;\n        }",
    "improvedCode": "        const redisValue = await this.redis.get(key);\n        if (redisValue) {\n            try {\n                const parsed = JSON.parse(redisValue) as T;\n                this.localCache.set(key, { value: parsed, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });\n                return parsed;\n            } catch (parseErr) {\n                this.logger.warn(`Corrupted cache value for key ${key}, treating as miss`);\n                await this.redis.del(key);\n            }\n        }",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Unhandled JSON.parse -- corrupted Redis data crashes the caller instead of falling through to fetchFn as a cache miss",

  {
    "id": "3",

#### src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts (symbol: UserRepository)

import { UserRepository } from '../repositories/user.repository';
import { db } from '../db';

export async function authenticateRequest(
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction,
) {
  const userRepo = new UserRepository(db);
  const result = await userRepo.findUserWithAccess(req.userId);

  if (!result) {
    return res.status(401).json({ error: 'User not found' });
  }

  req.user = result.user;
  req.userRoles = result.roles;

  if (!result.roles?.includes('admin') && req.path.startsWith('/admin')) {
    return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Admin access required' });
  }

  next();
}
```

import { UserRepository } from '../repositories/user.repository';
import { db } from '../db';

export class ProfileController {
  private userRepo = new UserRepository(db);

  async getProfile(req: Request, res: Response): Promise<void> {
    const user = await this.userRepo.findById(req.params.id);
    if (!user) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: 'User not found' });
      return;
    }
    res.json({
      id: user.id,
      email: user.email,
      name: user.fullName,
      joinedAt: user.createdAt,
    });
  }
}

import { UserRepository } from '../repositories/user.repository';
import { db } from '../db';

const MAX_TEAM_SIZE = 50;

export class TeamService {
  private userRepo = new UserRepository(db);

  async hasCapacity(teamId: string): Promise<boolean> {
    const team = await this.userRepo.getTeamMembers(teamId);
    return team.memberCount < MAX_TEAM_SIZE;
  }

  async addMember(teamId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
    const canAdd = await this.hasCapacity(teamId);
    if (!canAdd) {
      throw new Error(`Team ${teamId} is at maximum capacity (${MAX_TEAM_SIZE})`);
    }
    await this.memberRepo.add(teamId, userId);
  }
}
```

  private defaultTtl: number;

  constructor(redisUrl: string) {
    this.redis = new Redis(redisUrl);
    this.defaultTtl = CACHE_TTL;
  }

  async get<T>(key: string): Promise<T | null> {
    const data = await this.redis.get(key);
    return data ? JSON.parse(data) : null;
  }

  async set<T>(key: string, value: T, ttl: number = this.defaultTtl): Promise<void> {
    await this.redis.setex(key, ttl, JSON.stringify(value));
  }
}

import { MAX_RETRIES } from '../config/app-config';
import axios, { AxiosRequestConfig, AxiosResponse } from 'axios';

export class HttpClient {
  async requestWithRetry<T>(
    config: AxiosRequestConfig,
    retries: number = MAX_RETRIES,
  ): Promise<AxiosResponse<T>> {
    for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= retries; attempt++) {
      try {
        return await axios(config);
      } catch (err) {
        if (attempt === retries) throw err;
        await this.delay(Math.pow(2, attempt) * 1000);
      }
    }
    throw new Error('Unreachable');
  }

  private delay(ms: number): Promise<void> {
    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
  }
}
```

import { validateEmail } from '../utils/validation';

export class AuthController {
  async register(req: Request, res: Response): Promise<void> {
    const { email, password, name } = req.body;

    if (!validateEmail(email, true)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid email address' });
      return;
    }

    const user = await this.userService.createUser({ email, password, name });
    const token = this.tokenService.generateAccessToken(user.id);
    res.status(201).json({ user: { id: user.id, email }, token });
  }

  async login(req: Request, res: Response): Promise<void> {
    const { email, password } = req.body;
    if (!validateEmail(email, false)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid email format' });
      return;
    }
    const user = await this.userService.authenticate(email, password);
    if (!user) {
      res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' });
      return;
    }
    res.json({ token: this.tokenService.generateAccessToken(user.id) });
  }
}
```

#### src/middleware/input-sanitizer.ts (symbol: sanitizeInput)
**Rationale:** Find middleware using sanitizeInput to verify request pipeline impact

import { sanitizeInput } from '../utils/validation';

export function sanitizeBody(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
  if (req.body && typeof req.body.content === 'string') {
    req.body.content = sanitizeInput(req.body.content, 5000);
  }
  if (req.body && typeof req.body.title === 'string') {
    req.body.title = sanitizeInput(req.body.title, 200);
  }
  next();
}

export function sanitizeQueryParams(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(req.query)) {
    if (typeof value === 'string') {
      (req.query as any)[key] = sanitizeInput(value, 500);
    }
  }
  next();
}
```

import { parseDate, sanitizeInput } from '../utils/validation';

export class ImportService {
  async processRow(row: Record<string, string>): Promise<ProcessedRow> {
    const createdAt = parseDate(row.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    const updatedAt = row.updatedAt ? parseDate(row.updatedAt, 'ISO') : createdAt;
    const name = sanitizeInput(row.name, 255);

    return {
      name,
      createdAt,
      updatedAt,
      amount: parseFloat(row.amount),
      category: row.category || 'uncategorized',
    };
  }

  async importCsv(rows: Record<string, string>[]): Promise<ImportResult> {
    const processed = await Promise.all(rows.map(r => this.processRow(r)));
    return this.bulkInsert(processed);
  }
}

import { OrderStatus } from '../types/order-status';

export class OrderService {
  async handleNewOrder(orderId: string): Promise<void> {
    const order = await this.orderRepo.findById(orderId);

    switch (order.status) {
      case OrderStatus.PENDING_PAYMENT:
        await this.initiatePayment(order);
        break;
      case OrderStatus.PROCESSING:
        await this.assignFulfillmentCenter(order);
        break;
      default:
        throw new Error(`Unexpected order status: ${order.status}`);
    }
  }
}
```

import { OrderStatus } from '../types/order-status';

export class FulfillmentService {
  async processOrders(): Promise<void> {
    const orders = await this.orderRepo.findByStatus(OrderStatus.PROCESSING);
    for (const order of orders) {
      const packed = await this.packingService.pack(order);
      if (packed) {
        await this.orderRepo.updateStatus(order.id, OrderStatus.SHIPPED);
        await this.notificationService.sendShippedEmail(order.customerId);
      }
    }
  }
}

#### src/services/shipping.service.ts (symbol: OrderStatus)

import { OrderStatus } from '../types/order-status';

export class ShippingService {
  async getTrackingInfo(orderId: string): Promise<TrackingInfo | null> {
    const order = await this.orderRepo.findById(orderId);
    if (order.status !== OrderStatus.SHIPPED && order.status !== OrderStatus.DELIVERED) {
      return null;
    }
    return this.trackingProvider.lookup(order.trackingNumber);
  }

  async confirmDelivery(orderId: string): Promise<void> {
    const order = await this.orderRepo.findById(orderId);
    if (order.status !== OrderStatus.SHIPPED) {
      throw new Error('Can only confirm delivery for shipped orders');
    }
    await this.orderRepo.updateStatus(orderId, OrderStatus.DELIVERED);
  }
}
```

#### src/services/analytics.service.ts (symbol: OrderStatus)
**Rationale:** Find services calling OrderStatus to assess downstream impact
```
import { OrderStatus } from '../types/order-status';

export class AnalyticsService {
  async getCancellationRate(startDate: Date, endDate: Date): Promise<number> {
    const totalOrders = await this.orderRepo.countInRange(startDate, endDate);
    const cancelledOrders = await this.orderRepo.countByStatusInRange(
      OrderStatus.CANCELLED,
      startDate,
      endDate,
    );
    return totalOrders > 0 ? cancelledOrders / totalOrders : 0;
  }

  async getDashboardMetrics(): Promise<DashboardMetrics> {
    const processing = await this.orderRepo.countByStatus(OrderStatus.PROCESSING);
    const shipped = await this.orderRepo.countByStatus(OrderStatus.SHIPPED);
    const cancelled = await this.orderRepo.countByStatus(OrderStatus.CANCELLED);
    return { processing, shipped, cancelled };
  }
}
```

import { OrderStatus } from '../types/order-status';

export class RefundService {
  async processRefund(orderId: string): Promise<RefundResult> {
    const order = await this.orderRepo.findById(orderId);
    if (order.status !== OrderStatus.REFUND_REQUESTED) {
      throw new Error('Order is not in refund-requested state');
    }

    const refund = await this.paymentGateway.refund(order.chargeId, order.total);
    await this.orderRepo.updateStatus(orderId, OrderStatus.CANCELLED);
    await this.notificationService.sendRefundConfirmation(
      order.customerId,
      refund.id,
    );
    return { refundId: refund.id, amount: order.total };
  }

  async getRefundQueue(): Promise<Order[]> {
    return this.orderRepo.findByStatus(OrderStatus.REFUND_REQUESTED);
  }
}

30:     public boolean processPayment(String merchantId, BigDecimal amount, String currency) {
31:         if (amount.equals(BigDecimal.ZERO)) {
32:             logger.warn("Skipping zero-amount payment for merchant: {}", merchantId);
33:             return false;
34:         }
35: 
36:         BigDecimal convertedAmount = convertCurrency(amount, currency, "USD");
37:         logger.info("Processing payment of {} USD for merchant {}", convertedAmount, merchantId);
38: 
39:         String query = "SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE merchant_id = '" + merchantId + "'";
40:         try {
41:             Statement stmt = connection.createStatement();
42:             ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery(query);
43:             while (rs.next()) {
44:                 logger.debug("Found existing transaction: {}", rs.getString("id"));
45:             }
46:         } catch (Exception e) {
47:             logger.error("Failed to query transactions", e);
48:         }
49: 
50:         boolean success = gateway.charge(merchantId, convertedAmount);
51:         if (success) {
52:             logger.info("Payment succeeded for merchant {}", merchantId);
53:         }
54:         return success;
55:     }
56: 
57:     public List<PaymentSummary> getPaymentSummaries(List<Order> orders) {
58:         List<PaymentSummary> summaries = new ArrayList<>();
59:         for (Order order : orders) {
60:             Payment payment = orderRepository.findPaymentByOrderId(order.getId());
61:             if (payment != null) {
62:                 summaries.add(new PaymentSummary(order, payment));
63:             }
64:         }
65:         return summaries;
66:     }
67: 
68:     public boolean deductBalance(String accountId, BigDecimal amount) {
69:         BigDecimal currentBalance = orderRepository.getBalance(accountId);
70:         if (currentBalance.compareTo(amount) >= 0) {
71:             BigDecimal newBalance = currentBalance.subtract(amount);
72:             orderRepository.setBalance(accountId, newBalance);
73:             logger.info("Deducted {} from account {}, new balance: {}", amount, accountId, newBalance);
74:             return true;
75:         }
76:         logger.warn("Insufficient balance for account {}", accountId);
77:         return false;
78:     }
79: 
80:     private BigDecimal convertCurrency(BigDecimal amount, String from, String to) {
81:         if (from.equals(to)) {
82:             return amount;
83:         }
84:         BigDecimal rate = gateway.getExchangeRate(from, to);
85:         return amount.multiply(rate);
86:     }

32:     public AuthResult authenticate(String username, String password) {
33:         logger.info("Login attempt for user: " + username + " with password: " + password);
34: 
35:         if (isLockedOut(username)) {
36:             logger.warn("Account locked for user: {}", username);
37:             return AuthResult.locked();
38:         }
39: 
40:         User user = userRepository.findByUsername(username);
41:         if (user == null) {
42:             incrementAttempts(username);
43:             return AuthResult.failure("Invalid credentials");
44:         }
45: 
46:         String storedHash = user.getPasswordHash();
47:         String inputHash = hashPassword(password, user.getSalt());
48: 
49:         if (inputHash.equals(storedHash)) {
50:             loginAttempts.remove(username);
51:             String sessionId = UUID.randomUUID().toString();
52:             sessionStore.create(sessionId, user.getId(), Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600));
53:             logger.info("User {} authenticated successfully, session: {}", username, sessionId);
54:             return AuthResult.success(sessionId);
55:         } else {
56:             incrementAttempts(username);
57:             return AuthResult.failure("Invalid credentials");
58:         }
59:     }
60: 
61:     private void incrementAttempts(String username) {
62:         int current = loginAttempts.getOrDefault(username, 0);
63:         loginAttempts.put(username, current + 1);
64:         if (current + 1 >= MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS) {
65:             lockoutTimestamps.put(username, System.currentTimeMillis());
66:             logger.warn("Account {} locked after {} failed attempts", username, current + 1);
67:         }
68:     }
69: 
70:     private boolean isLockedOut(String username) {
71:         Long lockedAt = lockoutTimestamps.get(username);
72:         if (lockedAt == null) return false;
73:         if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lockedAt > LOCKOUT_DURATION_MS) {
74:             lockoutTimestamps.remove(username);
75:             loginAttempts.remove(username);
76:             return false;
77:         }
78:         return true;
79:     }

83:             MessageDigest md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");

    "suggestionContent": "SHA-256 is not a password hashing algorithm. The `hashPassword()` method at lines 81-94 uses `MessageDigest.getInstance(\"SHA-256\")` to hash passwords. SHA-256 is a general-purpose cryptographic hash designed to be fast -- modern GPUs can compute billions of SHA-256 hashes per second. An attacker with a database dump can brute-force the entire password space trivially. Password hashing must use a purpose-built slow algorithm like bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id that incorporates a configurable work factor. Concrete scenario: an attacker obtains the user table and cracks 90% of passwords within hours using hashcat on consumer hardware. Use `BCrypt.hashpw()` or similar instead.",

3: import java.util.HashMap;
4: import java.util.Map;
5: import java.util.Iterator;
6: import org.slf4j.Logger;
7: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
8: 
9: public class CacheManagerService {
10: 
11:     private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CacheManagerService.class);
12:     private static final long DEFAULT_TTL_MS = 300_000;
13: 
14:     private final Map<String, CacheEntry> cache = new HashMap<>();
15:     private final int maxSize;
16: 
17:     public CacheManagerService(int maxSize) {
18:         this.maxSize = maxSize;
19:     }
20: 
21:     public synchronized Object get(String key) {
22:         CacheEntry entry = cache.get(key);
23:         if (entry == null) {
24:             logger.debug("Cache miss for key: {}", key);
25:             return null;
26:         }
27:         if (System.currentTimeMillis() <= entry.getExpiresAt()) {
28:             logger.debug("Cache hit for key: {}", key);
29:             return entry.getValue();
30:         }
31:         cache.remove(key);
32:         logger.debug("Cache entry expired for key: {}", key);
33:         return null;
34:     }
35: 
36:     public void put(String key, Object value) {
37:         put(key, value, DEFAULT_TTL_MS);
38:     }
39: 
40:     public void put(String key, Object value, long ttlMs) {
41:         if (cache.size() >= maxSize) {
42:             evictExpired();
43:         }
44:         if (cache.size() >= maxSize) {
45:             String oldestKey = cache.keySet().iterator().next();
46:             cache.remove(oldestKey);
47:             logger.info("Evicted oldest entry: {}", oldestKey);
48:         }
49:         long expiresAt = System.currentTimeMillis() + ttlMs;
50:         cache.put(key, new CacheEntry(value, expiresAt));
51:     }
52: 
53:     public void invalidate(String key) {
54:         cache.remove(key);
55:     }
56: 
57:     public void clear() {
58:         cache.clear();
59:         logger.info("Cache cleared");
60:     }
61: 
62:     private void evictExpired() {
63:         long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
64:         for (Map.Entry<String, CacheEntry> entry : cache.entrySet()) {
65:             if (now > entry.getValue().getExpiresAt()) {
66:                 cache.remove(entry.getKey());
67:                 logger.debug("Evicted expired entry: {}", entry.getKey());
68:             }
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72:     public int size() {
73:         return cache.size();
74:     }
75: 
76:     private static class CacheEntry {
77:         private final Object value;
78:         private final long expiresAt;
79: 
80:         CacheEntry(Object value, long expiresAt) {
81:             this.value = value;
82:             this.expiresAt = expiresAt;
83:         }
84: 
85:         Object getValue() { return value; }
86:         long getExpiresAt() { return expiresAt; }
87:     }

70:     private String buildHeaderRow(List<Record> records) {
71:         if (records.isEmpty()) return "";
72:         return String.join(",", records.get(0).getColumnNames());

76:         if (records.isEmpty()) return new String[0];

80:     private String recordToCsvRow(Record record) {

85:             String val = values.get(i);

92:         return sb.toString();

40:     public void sendNotification(String userId, String templateName, Map<String, String> params) {
41:         User user = userRepository.findById(userId);
42:         String channel = user.getPreferences().getChannel();
43: 
44:         if (!rateLimiter.tryAcquire(userId)) {
45:             logger.warn("Rate limit exceeded for user: {}", userId);
46:             return;
47:         }
48: 
49:         String template = templateEngine.load(templateName);
50:         String body = template;
51:         for (Map.Entry<String, String> entry : params.entrySet()) {
52:             body = body.replace("{ {" + entry.getKey() + "} }", entry.getValue());
53:         }
54: 
55:         dispatchToChannel(channel, user, body);
56:     }
57: 
58:     private void dispatchToChannel(String channel, User user, String body) {
59:         ExecutorService executor = Executors.newSingleThreadExecutor();
60:         executor.submit(() -> {
61:             try {
62:                 switch (channel) {
63:                     case "email":
64:                         emailClient.send(user.getEmail(), "Notification", body);
65:                         break;
66:                     case "sms":
67:                         smsClient.send(user.getPhoneNumber(), body);
68:                         break;
69:                     case "push":
70:                         pushClient.send(user.getDeviceToken(), body);
71:                         break;
72:                     default:
73:                         logger.warn("Unknown channel: {}", channel);
74:                 }
75:                 logger.info("Notification sent to user {} via {}", user.getId(), channel);
76:             } catch (Exception e) {
77:                 /* retry later */
78:             }
79:         });
80:     }
81: 
82:     public void sendBulkNotification(String templateName, Map<String, String> params, String channel) {
83:         List<User> users = userRepository.findAll();
84:         for (User user : users) {
85:             try {
86:                 String template = templateEngine.load(templateName);

22: class CacheManager:
23:     """Thread-safe in-memory cache with TTL, LRU eviction, and DB fallback."""
24: 
25:     def __init__(self, db_client, max_size: int = 1000, default_ttl: float = 300.0):
26:         self.db = db_client
27:         self.max_size = max_size
28:         self.default_ttl = default_ttl
29:         self._cache: Dict[str, CacheEntry] = {}
30:         self._lock = threading.Lock()
31: 
32:     def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:
33:         """Retrieve a value from the cache, falling back to DB on miss."""
34:         with self._lock:
35:             if key in self._cache:
36:                 entry = self._cache[key]
37:                 elapsed = time.time() - entry.created_at
38:                 if elapsed < entry.ttl:
39:                     entry.last_access = time.time()
40:                     entry.access_count += 1
41:                     return entry.value
42:                 else:
43:                     del self._cache[key]
44: 
45:             # Cache miss -- fetch from database while holding the lock
46:             value = self.db.fetch(key)
47:             if value is not None:
48:                 self.set(key, value)
49:             return value
50: 
51:     def set(self, key: str, value: Any, ttl: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
52:         """Store a value in the cache."""
53:         if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:
54:             self._evict_expired()
55:         if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:
56:             self._evict_lru()
57:         effective_ttl = ttl if ttl is not None else self.default_ttl
58:         self._cache[key] = CacheEntry(key, value, effective_ttl)
59: 
60:     def __contains__(self, key: str) -> bool:
61:         """Check if a key is in the cache."""
62:         return key in self._cache
63: 
64:     def _evict_expired(self) -> None:
65:         """Remove all expired entries from the cache."""
66:         now = time.time()
67:         for key in self._cache:
68:             entry = self._cache[key]
69:             if now - entry.created_at >= entry.ttl:
70:                 del self._cache[key]
71: 
72:     def _evict_lru(self) -> None:
73:         """Evict the least recently used entry."""
74:         if not self._cache:

10 +class CacheEntry:

22 +class CacheManager:
23 +    """Thread-safe in-memory cache with TTL, LRU eviction, and DB fallback."""
24 +
25 +    def __init__(self, db_client, max_size: int = 1000, default_ttl: float = 300.0):
26 +        self.db = db_client
27 +        self.max_size = max_size
28 +        self.default_ttl = default_ttl
29 +        self._cache: Dict[str, CacheEntry] = {}
30 +        self._lock = threading.Lock()
31 +
32 +    def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:
33 +        """Retrieve a value from the cache, falling back to DB on miss."""
34 +        with self._lock:
35 +            if key in self._cache:
36 +                entry = self._cache[key]
37 +                elapsed = time.time() - entry.created_at
38 +                if elapsed < entry.ttl:
39 +                    entry.last_access = time.time()
40 +                    entry.access_count += 1
41 +                    return entry.value
42 +                else:
43 +                    del self._cache[key]
44 +
45 +            # Cache miss -- fetch from database while holding the lock
46 +            value = self.db.fetch(key)
47 +            if value is not None:
48 +                self.set(key, value)
49 +            return value
50 +
51 +    def set(self, key: str, value: Any, ttl: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
52 +        """Store a value in the cache."""
53 +        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:
54 +            self._evict_expired()
55 +        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:
56 +            self._evict_lru()
57 +        effective_ttl = ttl if ttl is not None else self.default_ttl
58 +        self._cache[key] = CacheEntry(key, value, effective_ttl)
59 +
60 +    def __contains__(self, key: str) -> bool:
61 +        """Check if a key is in the cache."""
62 +        return key in self._cache
63 +
64 +    def _evict_expired(self) -> None:
65 +        """Remove all expired entries from the cache."""
66 +        now = time.time()
67 +        for key in self._cache:
68 +            entry = self._cache[key]
69 +            if now - entry.created_at >= entry.ttl:
70 +                del self._cache[key]
71 +
72 +    def _evict_lru(self) -> None:
73 +        """Evict the least recently used entry."""
74 +        if not self._cache:
75 +            return
76 +        lru_key = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k].last_access)
77 +        del self._cache[lru_key]
78 +
79 +    def clear(self) -> None:
80 +        """Clear all entries."""
81 +        with self._lock:

89 +            if now - e.created_at >= e.ttl

6: import requests
7: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
8: 
9: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

11: 
12: class ApiClient:
13:     """HTTP API client with retry logic, authentication, and request signing."""
14: 
15:     def __init__(self, base_url: str, api_key: str, api_secret: str, max_retries: int = 3):
16:         self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
17:         self.api_key = api_key
18:         self.api_secret = api_secret
19:         self.max_retries = max_retries
20:         self.timeout = 30.0
21: 
22:     def get(self, endpoint: str, params: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
23:         """Perform a signed GET request with retry."""
24:         url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
25:         return self._request_with_retry('GET', url, params=params)
26: 
27:     def post(self, endpoint: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
28:         """Perform a signed POST request with retry."""
29:         url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
30:         return self._request_with_retry('POST', url, json_data=data)
31: 
32:     def _sign_request(self, method: str, url: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
33:         """Generate HMAC signature for request authentication."""
34:         message = f"{method}:{url}:{timestamp}"
35:         logger.debug(f"Request signed with key={self.api_key} secret={self.api_secret}")
36:         signature = hmac.new(
37:             self.api_secret.encode(),
38:             message.encode(),
39:             hashlib.sha256
40:         ).hexdigest()
41:         return signature
42: 
43:     def _request_with_retry(self, method: str, url: str,
44:                              params: Optional[Dict] = None,
45:                              json_data: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
46:         """Execute HTTP request with retry on failure."""
47:         timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
48:         signature = self._sign_request(method, url, timestamp)
49:         headers = {
50:             'X-API-Key': self.api_key,
51:             'X-Timestamp': timestamp,
52:             'X-Signature': signature,
53:         }
54: 
55:         for i in range(self.max_retries):
56:             try:
57:                 if method == 'GET':
58:                     response = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers,
59:                                             timeout=self.timeout)
60:                 elif method == 'POST':
61:                     response = requests.post(url, json=json_data, headers=headers,
62:                                              timeout=self.timeout)
63:                 else:
64:                     raise ValueError(f"Unsupported method: {method}")
65: 
66:                 if response.elapsed.total_seconds() == 30.0:
67:                     logger.warning("Request timed out")
68: 
69:                 response.raise_for_status()
70:                 return response.json()
71: 
72:             except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
73:                 logger.warning(f"Request attempt {i + 1} failed: {e}")
74:                 continue
75: 
76:     def health_check(self) -> bool:
77:         """Check if the API is reachable."""
78:         try:
79:             response = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}/health", timeout=5)
80:             return response.status_code == 200
81:         except requests.ConnectionError:
82:             return False
83: 
84:     def close(self) -> None:
85:         """Cleanup client resources."""
86:         logger.info("ApiClient closed")

24: 
25:     def __init__(self, dsn: str, min_conn: int = 2, max_conn: int = 10):

28: 
29:     def run_query(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> List[Row]:

31:         conn = self.pool.getconn()
32:         try:
33:             logger.debug(f"Executing: {sql} with {params}")
34:             cursor = conn.cursor()
35:             cursor.execute(sql, params)
36:             columns = [desc[0] for desc in cursor.description]
37:             rows = []
38:             for row_data in cursor.fetchall():
39:                 row_dict = dict(zip(columns, row_data))
40:                 rows.append(Row(data=row_dict, columns=columns))
41:             conn.commit()
42:             return rows
43:         except Exception as e:
44:             conn.rollback()
45:             logger.error(f"Query failed: {e}")

33 +class EventEmitter:
34 +    """Async event system with typed events."""
35 +
36 +    def __init__(self):
37 +        self._listeners: Dict[Type[Event], List[Callable]] = {}
38 +        self._global_listeners: List[Callable] = []
39 +

41 +        """Register a typed event listener."""

50 +            self._listeners[event_class].remove(callback)
51 +            logger.debug(f"Removed listener for {event_class.__name__}")
52 +

54 +        """Register a listener for all events."""
55 +        self._global_listeners.append(callback)
56 +
57 +    async def emit(self, event: Event) -> None:
58 +        """Emit a typed event to registered listeners."""

61 +        all_handlers = handlers + self._global_listeners
62 +
63 +        logger.info(f"Emitting {event_class.__name__} to {len(all_handlers)} handlers")

import json
from src.utils.serializer import serialize

class CacheService:
    def __init__(self, redis_client):
        self.redis = redis_client

    def cache_object(self, key: str, obj: dict, ttl: int = 300):
        """Cache an object by serializing it."""
        data = serialize(obj)
        # Assumes data is a JSON string, stores in Redis
        self.redis.setex(key, ttl, data)

    def get_cached(self, key: str) -> dict:
        raw = self.redis.get(key)
        return json.loads(raw)

```

from src.utils.serializer import deserialize

class QueueConsumer:
    def __init__(self, queue_client):
        self.queue = queue_client

    def process_message(self, message: dict):
        """Process a message from the queue."""
        body = message['body']  # This is a JSON string from the queue
        data = deserialize(body)
        return self._handle(data)

    def process_batch(self, messages: list):
        results = []
        for msg in messages:
            results.append(self.process_message(msg))
        return results

Every other public method (`get`, `set`, `fetch`, `delete`, `clear`, `cleanup_expired`) acquires `@mutex` before touching `@store`, but `get_or_set` bypasses the lock entirely. This means a thread calling `get_or_set` can read or write `@store` concurrently with another thread that holds the mutex in `set` or `cleanup_expired`. In CRuby, the GIL provides some safety for hash reads, but in JRuby or TruffleRuby (which have true parallelism), this is a data race that can corrupt the hash's internal structure. 6: class NotificationDispatcher
7:   RATE_LIMIT = 100 # max notifications per minute
8: 
9:   def initialize(email_client, sms_client, push_client, logger)
10:     @email_client = email_client
11:     @sms_client = sms_client
12:     @push_client = push_client
13:     @logger = logger
14:     @sent_count = 0
15:     @last_reset = Time.now
16:   end
17: 
18:   def dispatch(user, event, template_str = nil)
19:     return { success: false, error: 'rate_limited' } if rate_limited?
20: 
21:     channel = resolve_channel(user)
22:     return { success: false, error: 'no_channel' } unless channel
23: 
24:     message = build_message(event, template_str, user)
25: 
26:     result = case channel
27:     when :email
28:       send_email(user, message)
29:     when :sms
30:       send_sms(user, message)
31:     when :push
32:       send_push(user, message)
33:     else
34:       { success: false, error: 'unknown_channel' }
35:     end
36: 
37:     @sent_count += 1
38:     @logger.info("Notification sent via #{channel} to user #{user.id}")
39:     result
40:   end
41: 
42:   def broadcast(event, template_str = nil)
43:     users = User.all.select { |u| u.subscribed? }
44:     results = []
45: 
46:     users.each do |user|
47:       results << dispatch(user, event, template_str)
48:     end
49: 
50:     {
51:       total: users.size,
52:       sent: results.count { |r| r[:success] },
53:       failed: results.count { |r| !r[:success] }
54:     }
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def resolve_channel(user)
60:     preferred = user.preferences.notification_channel
61: 
62:     if preferred == :email && user.email.present?
63:       :email
64:     elsif preferred == :sms && user.phone.present?
65:       :sms
66:     elsif preferred == :push && user.device_token.present?
67:       :push
68:     else
69:       nil
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   def build_message(event, template_str, user)
74:     template = template_str || default_template(event)
75:     ERB.new(template).result(binding)
76:   end
77: 
78:   def default_template(event)
79:     "Hello, you have a new notification: #{event.to_s}"
80:   end
81: 
82:   def send_email(user, message)
83:     @email_client.deliver(
84:       to: user.email,
85:       subject: 'Notification',
86:       body: message
87:     )
88:     { success: true, channel: :email }
89:   end
90: 
91:   def send_sms(user, message)
92:     phone = user.phone.gsub(/[^0-9+]/, '')
93:     @sms_client.send(to: phone, body: message)
94:     { success: true, channel: :sms }
95:   end
96: 
97:   def send_push(user, message)
98:     @push_client.notify(token: user.device_token, body: message)
99:     { success: true, channel: :push }
100:   end
101: 
102:   def rate_limited?
103:     if Time.now - @last_reset > 60
104:       @sent_count = 0
105:       @last_reset = Time.now
106:     end
107: 
108:     @sent_count >= RATE_LIMIT
109:   end
110: end

      uncached_ids = ids.reject { |id| @cache.key?(id) }
      # Single query for uncached
      if uncached_ids.any?
        records = model_class.where(id: uncached_ids)
        records.each { |r| @cache[r.id] = r }
      end
      ids.map { |id| @cache[id] }
    end",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "batch_find executes N+1 individual queries instead of a single WHERE id IN query",
